overview--docs.name: Docs
overview--docs.title: Overview
overview--docs.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import logo from './CourtHive.png';

CourtHive Components

CourtHive Components is a framework-agnostic UI library for tournament and competition management. From draw bracket rendering and live scoring to visual topology design and schedule optimization, these components provide the building blocks for rich competition interfaces — all built on vanilla HTML/CSS/JS with no framework lock-in. Powered by{' '} Competition Factory{' '} for TODS-compliant tournament data modeling and business logic.

Component Categories

Draws

Tournament draw rendering and interaction.

StoryDescription
StructureFull draw structure rendering with bracket connectors, participant names, scores, and seed positions. Supports multiple visual compositions (themes).
RoundsIndividual round rendering within a draw structure.
Round VisibilityControls for showing/hiding specific rounds in a draw.
Participant AssignmentDrag-and-drop and interactive assignment of participants to draw positions.
ScratchExperimental draw rendering workspace.

MatchUps

Match-level rendering and scoring.

StoryDescription
MatchUpIndividual matchUp card rendering with participants, scores, schedule info, and status indicators.
SidesParticipant side rendering within a matchUp (names, seeds, nationality flags).
Game ScorePoint-by-point game score display.
TickCompact tick/check indicator for matchUp completion status.
StatusStatus pill badges for matchUp states (upcoming, in progress, complete, etc.).

Participants

Participant display components.

StoryDescription
ParticipantParticipant name rendering with seed numbers, entry status, and rankings.
IndividualIndividual participant detail card.
FlagCountry/nationality flag rendering using IOC and ISO codes.
FrillDecorative elements for participant display (seed badges, entry indicators).

Components

Interactive UI components and complex widgets.

StoryDescription
TopologyBuilderVisual editor for designing tournament draw topologies. Add structures (Main, Qualifying, Consolation, Playoff), connect them with winner/loser/position links, validate, and generate draw options. Includes templates for common patterns (SE, FMLC, FIC, Compass, etc.).
ScoringScoring modal with multiple input approaches (buttons, gamepad, keyboard). Configurable for different match formats and sports.
MatchUpFormatMatchUp format code editor for defining scoring rules (sets, games, tiebreaks, points).
ModalGeneral-purpose modal dialog with configurable content, buttons, info panels, and styling.
DrawerSlide-in panel (left/right) for forms, configuration, and detail views.
ControlBarToolbar with search, filters, action buttons, and tabulator table integration.
FlightProfileFlight/section configuration for splitting draws into multiple flights.
CategoriesAge category and tournament category configuration editors.
MockParticipantsTest data generator for creating realistic participant records with configurable age ranges, genders, and nationalities.
TipsterDropdown suggestion/autocomplete component for search and selection.

Renderers

Low-level rendering utilities for building custom UIs.

StoryDescription
FormDeclarative form renderer from field configuration arrays. Supports text, select, checkbox, radio, and custom controls with validation and relationships.
FieldIndividual form field renderer.
ButtonsButton bar renderer with configurable styles, icons, and click handlers.
MenuContext menu and dropdown menu renderer.

Visualizations

Data visualization components.

StoryDescription
Burst ChartSunburst/radial chart for visualizing tournament draw progressions, bracket paths, and hierarchical competition data.

Scheduling Profile

Venue and court scheduling configuration.

StoryDescription
FullComplete scheduling profile editor with venue selection, court assignment, and time slot configuration.
PanelsIndividual scheduling profile panels (venue, court, time slot editors).
FactoryFactory-backed scheduling profile with live tournament engine integration.

Temporal Grid

Timeline-based scheduling and conflict detection.

StoryDescription
TimelineInteractive CourtTimeline for visualizing and editing court schedules across time.
EngineTemporal grid scheduling engine with automatic matchUp placement.
Conflict EvaluatorsConflict detection strategies for identifying scheduling issues (participant conflicts, rest time violations, venue capacity).

Architecture

{`src/
  components/              Component source code
    topology-builder/        Controller / Domain / Engine / UI layers
    scheduling-profile/      Controller / Domain / Engine / UI layers
    temporal-grid/           Controller / Engine / UI layers
    scoring/                 Approaches / Logic / Utils
    renderStructure/         Draw bracket rendering
    renderSchematicStructure/  Compact schematic previews
    forms/                   Declarative form system
    ...
  compositions/            Visual theme definitions
  styles/                  CSS custom properties and base styles
  stories/                 Storybook stories`}

Key Patterns

  • Framework-agnostic — All components render to vanilla DOM elements. No React, Vue, or Angular dependency.
  • CSS custom properties — Theming via --chc-* CSS variables with light/dark mode support.
  • Factory integration — Tournament logic powered by Competition Factory for TODS-compliant data modeling.
  • Controller pattern — Complex features (topology builder, scheduling profile, temporal grid) use a controller layer that wires store, domain logic, and UI panels together.

Getting Started

{`# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Start Storybook
pnpm storybook

# Build the library
pnpm build`}

Usage

{`import {
  renderStructure,
  scoringModal,
  TopologyBuilderControl,
} from 'courthive-components';
import 'courthive-components/dist/courthive-components.css';`}
Repository:{' '} github.com/CourtHive/courthive-components
Factory docs:{' '} courthive.github.io/competition-factory
